entries.claim: Codec safety and deterministic decode are guarded by required tests and generated roundtrips.
entries.evidence: Release gates require cargo test --all-targets plus generated compress/decompress scripts.
entries.limitation: This ledger records the gate requirement; command output is verified separately by the release gate run.

entries.claim: Planted short-seed data can produce negative delta.
entries.evidence: planted-sha256-arity2: 256 -> 168 bytes (-34.38%).
entries.limitation: Planted data proves mechanism, not natural corpus prevalence.

entries.claim: Indexed lookup and streaming lookup agree on the planted span8 fixture.
entries.evidence: indexed/span8 and streaming/span8 both output 720 bytes.
entries.limitation: Small fixture parity; deeper seed spaces still need larger parity matrices.

entries.claim: Record overhead threshold is visible and span length matters.
entries.evidence: span-4 bloats by 83 bytes; span-20 saves 682 bytes.
entries.limitation: Generated spans only; not a general compression promise.

entries.claim: Planted-density break-even is measured.
entries.evidence: First negative density row is density-256 at 256 planted bytes.
entries.limitation: Break-even depends on record format, span length, and corpus construction.

entries.claim: Recursive v2 can create a second-pass win, but does not show open-ended convergence.
entries.evidence: offset-1 pass1 1108 bytes, pass2 759 bytes, pass4 759 bytes.
entries.limitation: Current positive recursive evidence is an offset/control fixture.

entries.claim: Sub-block span-step search can recover offset generated spans in one pass.
entries.evidence: offset-1 block-step selected 0 spans; span-step-1 offsets 1, 2, and 3 output 724 bytes with 128 spans, 725 bytes with 128 spans, 726 bytes with 128 spans.
entries.limitation: Byte-step search expands the candidate grid and cost; it is alignment evidence, not a natural-corpus result.

entries.claim: Seed depth 2 expands the searchable space and is measured separately from normal fast tests.
entries.evidence: two-byte planted fixture depth1 1107 bytes with 0 spans; depth2 848 bytes with 128 spans; structured depth2 1.22% delta.
entries.limitation: Depth 2 proves wider search can unlock controlled seeds, but current structured depth-2 control still does not shrink.

entries.claim: Seed depth 3 is measured as an opt-in deep-search artifact.
entries.evidence: three-byte planted fixture depth2 1107 bytes with 0 spans; depth3 976 bytes with 128 spans; structured depth3 1.22% delta.
entries.limitation: Depth 3 is only proven on tiny opt-in controls; current structured depth-3 control still does not shrink.

entries.claim: Cheap generic reversible transforms do not create structured JSON seed-span wins yet.
entries.evidence: identity 1.22%, xor-prev 1.23%, sub-prev 1.23%, line-transpose 3.61%; all selected 0 spans.
entries.limitation: This covers only cheap byte-layout transforms on one generated JSON corpus.

entries.claim: A static domain-token preconditioner can reduce effective JSON bytes, but it is transform gain rather than seed-span gain.
entries.evidence: static-token effective delta -68.39% with 0 selected spans.
entries.limitation: This is a domain dictionary experiment, not current `.tlmr` format support and not evidence of natural seed matches.

entries.claim: Structured and binary controls do not yet show natural-corpus wins.
entries.evidence: structured JSON 1.22% delta; best structured-search sweep structured-json-span8-step4-pass1 1.22% delta; best broad corpus-matrix row http-headers-depth2 0.18% delta with 0 selected spans; structured depth3 1.22% delta; kolyma PDF 0.0077% delta.
entries.limitation: This is honest negative evidence across current seed-depth-1, seed-depth-2, broad generated corpus-matrix, and tiny opt-in seed-depth-3 structured searches; transformed wins remain open.

entries.claim: Composing context/residual transforms with periodic masks does not yet cross the fifth-byte gate.
entries.evidence: 525 composed candidates selected 33 discovery leads; held-out prefix>=5 uplift 0 corpora; held-out exact hits 0.
entries.limitation: Composition is a bounded research probe, not current `.tlmr` format support.

entries.claim: Depth-3 search can move a few held-out prefix frontiers, but exact hits are still absent.
entries.evidence: 16843008 depth-3 seeds enumerated; 3 held-out rows gained prefix>=5 movement; exact hits 0.
entries.limitation: Prefix movement is a search-frontier signal, not a `.tlmr` compression win until exact seed-span records beat overhead.

entries.claim: The bounded depth-3 compression follow-up did not convert prefix movement into selected spans.
entries.evidence: 2 physical input / 3 logical aliases; selected spans 0; negative-delta rows 0.
entries.limitation: This is a useful null on the current promoted rows, not a proof that all depth-3 search is barren.

entries.claim: Context-conditioned fifth-byte steering does not yet beat the held-out promotion gate.
entries.evidence: 16 contextual masks checked; held-out prefix>=5 win rows 0; exact-hit rows 0; null prefix>=5 win rows 0.
entries.limitation: This rejects one plausible steering family; it does not rule out richer reversible transforms.

entries.claim: Bounded structural reversible transforms do not yet create held-out longer-prefix or exact seed-span wins.
entries.evidence: 30 structural candidates over 660 validation rows; held-out prefix>=5 win corpora 0; held-out exact hits 0; shadow prefix>=5 win corpora 0; binary exact hits 0.
entries.limitation: This rejects the current bounded structural families, not all possible reversible preconditioners.

entries.claim: Affine byte remaps can move held-out prefix-4 proximity broadly, but still do not cross the fifth-byte gate.
entries.evidence: 7937 affine candidates searched; 16 held-out corpora had prefix>=4 uplift; held-out prefix>=5 win corpora 0; held-out exact hits 0.
entries.limitation: Prefix-4 uplift is a steering signal, not a seed-span compression win.

entries.claim: Residual sidecar steering can force some exact spans, but current span-8 economics still bloat.
entries.evidence: 31 held-out rows had forced exact spans; 31 had positive seed contribution before sidecar cost; held-out positive net-delta rows 0; best held-out net delta 5 bytes.
entries.limitation: This measures span-8 suffix-residual sidecars only; it is not format support or a proof about all longer-span sidecars.

entries.claim: Longer-span residual sidecars need stronger evidence than the current prefix frontier provides.
entries.evidence: 648 break-even rows; minimum raw-suffix strict prefix 6; max observed held-out forced prefix 4; raw-suffix model-viable rows at observed prefix 0; sublinear model-viable rows at observed prefix 23; promoted rows 0.
entries.limitation: This is math-only gating; sublinear residual models need measured, invertible payload compression before format or engine work.

entries.claim: Measured residual payload coders expose one narrow held-out sidecar signal, but not general format support.
entries.evidence: 31 held-out payload rows; measured held-out negative rows 1; best measured case rust-like-heldout::affine-a003-b211::prefix4-suffix-xor; zlib best held-out net delta -101 bytes; quarter-rate held-out negative rows 0.
entries.limitation: This promotes a narrow experimental payload descriptor follow-up; it is not `.tlmr` sidecar format support.

entries.claim: The first experimental sidecar descriptor decodes, rejects corruption, and still bloats after full-stream overhead.
entries.evidence: 2 descriptor rows; decode verified rows 2; full-stream negative rows 0; best full-stream delta 596 bytes; best local selected delta -100 bytes.
entries.limitation: This falsifies immediate sidecar format promotion for the current row; lower-overhead records or larger bundles remain open.

entries.claim: Packed sidecar offset/seed-index budgets can make the promoted row full-stream negative on paper.
entries.evidence: 14 layout rows; negative layout rows 6; best safe layout delta_offset_u8_seed_u16 at -489 bytes; best layout delta_offset_u8_seed_u16_no_hashes at -553 bytes.
entries.limitation: This is a budget model only; it needs an exact packed-table decoder before it becomes evidence for format work.

entries.claim: A packed offset/seed-index sidecar descriptor can decode and produce full-stream negative delta on one promoted held-out row.
entries.evidence: 2 packed descriptor rows; decode verified rows 2; full-stream negative rows 2; best coder zlib_level9 at -485 bytes.
entries.limitation: This is one promoted held-out row and research-only descriptor evidence, not a general natural-corpus or `.tlmr` format claim.

entries.claim: Packed sidecar controls show the descriptor signal is real but still narrow.
entries.evidence: 96 control rows; encoded rows 10; unique negative cases 4; ordinary held-out negative cases 1; best delta -485 bytes.
entries.limitation: Current packed descriptor assumptions skip most selected rows and have only one ordinary held-out negative case.

entries.claim: Generalized packed offset and seed modes improve descriptor economics, but do not yet broaden ordinary held-out wins.
entries.evidence: 1920 generalized rows; encoded rows 1576; unique encoded source rows 48; unique negative cases 14; ordinary held-out negative cases 1; best-supported table bytes 2842 versus baseline 6872.
entries.limitation: This improves descriptor applicability, not proof of broad natural-corpus viability.

entries.claim: Frozen packed sidecar replication did not reproduce full-stream negative rows on unrelated held-out corpora.
entries.evidence: 20 predeclared corpora; 520 source cases; 6240 descriptor rows; decode verified rows 6240; full-stream negative rows 0; ordinary held-out negative groups 0.
entries.limitation: This is falsification evidence for the current descriptor and transform set; it does not rule out future match-discovery or deeper-search approaches.

entries.claim: Pre-sidecar match discovery did not find exact seed-span matches across the current validation and replication corpus matrix.
entries.evidence: 42 corpora; 1050 rows; 11154720 target spans scanned; rows with prefix>=5 0; rows with exact hits 0; selected spans 0.
entries.limitation: This covers seed depth 2, arity 1..5, and the current search policies; it does not rule out deeper seed windows, new corpora, or new reversible transforms.

entries.claim: Alignment and arity discovery found only unprofitable short-span exact hits.
entries.evidence: 25210986 target spans; 194 supported configs; skipped configs 11; rows with prefix>=5 0; exact hits 3480; positive exact hits 0; selected spans 0.
entries.limitation: This tests block size, step policy, phase, and arity at seed depth 2; it does not rule out deeper seed spaces or new reversible transforms.

entries.claim: Frozen reversible transforms did not create selected exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 16494870 transformed target spans; 21 transforms; rows with prefix>=5 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0; metadata-profitable rows 0.
entries.limitation: This covers the frozen transform-validation matrix at seed depth 2 and span length 8; it does not rule out new transform families or deeper seed spaces.

entries.claim: Selected affine, periodic, and composed leads did not create exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 71477770 selected-lead target spans; 91 leads; rows with prefix>=5 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0; metadata-profitable rows 0.
entries.limitation: This follows only already-selected affine, periodic, and composed leads; it is a falsifier for broadening current prefix-4 leads, not for all possible transforms.

entries.claim: Current CPU streaming scales to the generated 16 MiB planted-density sweep.
entries.limitation: 16 MiB is useful local evidence, not production-scale archival proof.

entries.claim: The generated scale-performance report makes bounded planted-density scaling interpretable but memory-heavy.
entries.evidence: 9 memory-scaling rows; largest scale 16.0 MiB; largest peak memory 1934.602 MiB; largest peak/estimated-table ratio 21.985; plateau ratio spread 6.514%; next double peak estimate 3869.204 MiB.
entries.limitation: This is planted-density scale telemetry only; it does not prove natural-corpus compression or production memory behavior.

entries.claim: The Tauri evidence ledger panel has static UI/bridge workflow coverage.
entries.evidence: UI evidence keys 87; Tauri evidence fields 115; required cards 22; missing Tauri fields 0; missing mock fields 0; claim level ui_workflow_static_smoke_passed.
entries.limitation: This is static schema and artifact wiring coverage; it is not a full desktop browser smoke run.

entries.claim: Selected-lead depth-3 compression follow-up stayed null after prefix movement.
entries.evidence: Lead depth-3 prefix probe enumerated 16843008 seeds; 2 rows gained prefix>=5 movement, exact hits 0. The follow-up ran 1 physical input / 2 aliases with 0 selected spans.
entries.limitation: Selected-lead prefix movement is still not a compression win without exact records selected by the compressor.

entries.claim: Depth-3 frontier exact discovery found prefix movement only, not exact seed-span records.
entries.evidence: 26 frontier rows over 15 physical payloads enumerated 16843008 seeds; prefix>=5 uplift rows 2, exact hits 0, selected spans 0.
entries.limitation: This is the current gate against broad depth-3/depth-4 work; it can be reopened only by stronger exact-hit evidence.

entries.claim: Depth-4 search is sharded and explicitly gated rather than a default next step.
entries.evidence: Depth-4 plan status gated; estimated incremental full depth-4 time 7.8799 hours; exact-8 probability on the current frontier 0.00017141.
entries.limitation: The plan makes depth-4 reproducible and sharded, but current evidence does not justify running all shards.

entries.claim: Depth-4 pilot shards are bounded evidence gates, not production compression claims.
entries.evidence: 1 pilot shard enumerated 16777216 four-byte seeds over 736265 frontier target spans; prefix>=5 rows 0; prefix>=6 rows 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0.
entries.limitation: One shard is useful null evidence only; the remaining shards stay gated until stronger frontier evidence appears.

entries.claim: The generated search-frontier gate blocks broad raw depth search from the current prefix-only frontier.
entries.evidence: Gate status hold-broad-depth-search; best non-planted forecast 828.0 GiB per expected exact hit; depth-4 exact-8 probability 0.00017141; selected span total 0; blocking gates 7 of 7.
entries.limitation: This is a decision artifact assembled from current evidence, not a mathematical impossibility proof or a compression result.

entries.claim: The generated mechanism-experiment ranking makes seed-table preset probing the next non-depth experiment.
entries.evidence: Top lane seed-table-preset-probe; top next artifact docs/SEED_TABLE_PRESET_PROBE.md; ready lanes 7; gated lanes 1; blocked lanes 2; selected spans 0.
entries.limitation: This is generated evidence triage, not natural-corpus compression proof or format support.

entries.claim: The generated long-span bundle gate blocks broad long-span sweeps from the current evidence base.
entries.evidence: Gates met 0 of 8; recommendation hold_long_span_bundle_sweeps; best forecast 828.0 GiB; selected spans 0; raw-suffix required prefix 6; max observed prefix 4; claim level long_span_bundle_gate_blocked_by_evidence.
entries.limitation: This is a no-run gate assembled from current evidence; it should be regenerated if any prerequisite artifact changes.

entries.claim: The generated seed-table preset probe did not yet promote a public Lotus preset.
entries.evidence: Canonical selected spans 37768; ordinary held-out negative groups 0; control negative groups 0; SHA-256 baseline selected spans 0; promotion met False.
entries.limitation: This is a research-only seed-table/Lotus preset probe, not `.tlmr` format support or natural-corpus proof.

entries.claim: The generated exact short-hit bundle economics artifact does not yet promote span-3 hit bundling.
entries.evidence: Reconstructed exact hits 3480; zero-overhead best delta -1410 bytes; full-stream negative rows 6; ordinary negative groups 3; control negative groups 1; control density comparable True; promotion met False.
entries.limitation: This is an accounting artifact over frozen verified short hits, not new search, `.tlmr` support, or broad natural-corpus proof.

entries.claim: The generated whole-stream residual vector probe closes the current residual-sidecar path as a broad compression claim.
entries.evidence: Rows 2970; honest encoded rows 2358; exact decode rows 2358; honest full-stream negative rows 0; ordinary held-out negative groups 0; control negative groups 0; measured residual coding near entropy bound True; promotion met False.
entries.limitation: This is a whole-stream residual-vector falsification artifact, not `.tlmr` format support, compressed literal-stream evidence, or a new seed search.

entries.claim: The generated expander salt ensemble did not beat the matched random-trial baseline.
entries.evidence: Predeclared salts 16; salted exact hits 0; salted expected exact hits 0.648961; random-trial multiplier exceeded False; salted selected-span rows 0; full-stream negative rows 0; ordinary negative groups 0; control negative groups 0; promotion met False.
entries.limitation: This is a research-only predeclared salt/preset ensemble probe, not `.tlmr` format support or evidence for per-file trained expanders.

entries.claim: The generated schema-native public dictionary probe found a narrow public-preset positive result.
entries.evidence: Public entries 101; family selected spans 11302; ordinary held-out negative groups 14; family control negative groups 0; wrong-schema negative groups 0; same-size random negative groups 0; shadow negative groups 0; beats generic baseline True; promotion met True.
entries.limitation: This is public dictionary-preset evidence on schema-shaped synthetic corpora, not current `.tlmr` format support or proof that SHA-256 seed expansion discovered natural structure.

entries.claim: The schema-native public dictionary replication blocked promotion on paired shadow controls.
entries.evidence: Replication corpora 20; standards selected spans 20239; ordinary negative groups 9; standards control negative groups 2; generic ordinary negative groups 2; wrong-family ordinary negative groups 0; claim level control_failed_on_frozen_expansion_corpora; promotion met False.
entries.limitation: This hardening artifact downgrades the public dictionary signal until paired shadow controls and externally sourced corpora separate cleanly.

entries.claim: The generated superposition telemetry proves selector auditability on deterministic overlap fixtures.
entries.evidence: Fixtures 4; candidates 13; retained alternatives 2; weighted extra savings 4; weighted-beats-greedy fixtures 1; unexplained discards 0; promotion met True.
entries.limitation: This is selector correctness and telemetry evidence only; it does not create new seed hits or prove natural-corpus compression.

entries.claim: Recursive v2 later-layer gains remain unpromoted outside planted offset controls.
entries.evidence: Fixtures 5; ordinary structured fixtures 3; ordinary later-win families 0; planted offset later-win families 0; first-pass control win families 1; all decoded exactly True; claim level recursive_structured_fixtures_not_promoted; promotion met False.
entries.limitation: This is real CLI v2 recursive evidence with metadata charged; it blocks broad recursive claims until at least two ordinary non-offset families improve on later layers.

entries.claim: Corpus generalization controls did not produce exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 6 controls scanned 71250 spans; prefix>=5 rows 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0.
entries.limitation: This reduces literal-token overfit risk but does not replace broader held-out natural corpus validation.

entries.claim: Byte-permutation seed-manifold alignment did not yet produce exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 7 transforms over 154 rows scanned 3046715 spans; prefix>=5 rows 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0; rows negative after metadata 0.
entries.limitation: This covers rank-based global and phase-local byte permutations only; grammar-channel transforms are tested separately.

entries.claim: BWT/MTF classic preconditioners did not yet produce exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 9 BWT/MTF/RLE transforms over 90 rows scanned 1939897 spans; prefix>=5 rows 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0; rows negative after metadata 0; shorter transformed payload rows 6.
entries.limitation: This covers bounded block BWT, MTF, and zero-run packing only; transform-only shortening is not counted as Lotus evidence.

entries.claim: Grammar/channel discovery did not yet produce exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 9 channels over 198 rows scanned 3480293 spans; prefix>=5 rows 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0; rows negative after metadata 0.
entries.limitation: This covers first-pass grammar channels with charged literal sidecars only; richer parsers may need a new artifact.

entries.claim: Numeric value-channel discovery did not yet produce exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 7 numeric value channels over 98 rows parsed 120214 values and scanned 618367 spans; prefix>=5 rows 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0; rows negative after metadata 0.
entries.limitation: This covers bounded parsed numeric value streams with exact reconstruction metadata charged; timestamp/vector numeric channels remain future research.

entries.claim: Held-out corpus expansion broadened deterministic corpus coverage but did not change the raw seed frontier.
entries.evidence: 20 frozen replication corpora, 13 ordinary and 7 controls, scanned 680395 raw spans; missing from corpus matrix 20; prefix>=5 rows 0; exact-hit rows 0; selected-span rows 0.
entries.limitation: This is a separate frontier artifact; it intentionally does not stale the expensive transform-validation or corpus-matrix hash chain.

entries.claim: Record/context-aware transforms did not yet produce exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 14 transforms over 84 rows scanned 1164215 spans; prefix>=5 rows 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0; rows negative after metadata 0.
entries.limitation: This covers the current line/field/fixed-width/delta descriptor families only, not every possible record-aware transform.

entries.claim: Token/dictionary transforms did not yet produce exact seed-span rows.
entries.evidence: 9 transforms over 126 rows scanned 827371 spans; prefix>=5 rows 0; exact hits 0; selected spans 0; rows negative after metadata 0.
entries.limitation: This covers lexeme dictionary and token-order streams only; richer grammar coders remain open research.

open_questions: Turn promising preconditioners into a versioned reversible format layer, or reject them as non-Telomere transform-only gains.
Find repeatable natural or transformed structured-corpus seed-span wins beyond the current seed-depth-1, seed-depth-2, and opt-in seed-depth-3 controls.
Find a transform family that survives held-out composition testing into prefix>=5 or exact seed-span hits.
Design transform families beyond the current bounded structural permutations and residuals, or retire this lane if new families keep returning null.
Find new independent match-discovery leads beyond the current depth-2 arity 1..5 validation and replication matrix.
Use depth-3 frontier exact discovery as the gate before any broad depth-3 or opt-in depth-4 search.
Use the depth-4 shard plan only for pilot shards until the promotion gate is met.
Use the generated search-frontier gate as the go/no-go source before any broad raw depth or full depth-4 execution.
Use the generated mechanism-experiment ranking to prioritize seed-table preset probing before raw-depth escalation or format promotion.
Use the generated long-span bundle gate before running long-span sweeps or optimizing bundle packing.
Use the generated seed-table preset probe as null evidence before spending further on public corpus-shaped Lotus presets.
Use the generated exact short-hit bundle economics artifact as null evidence before spending further on span-3 hit bundling.
Use the generated recursive structured fixtures as the gate before claiming recursive convergence beyond planted offset controls.
Use the generated scale-performance report before extending planted-density runs beyond the current 16 MiB memory-heavy sweep.
Keep the generated UI workflow smoke in lockstep with any Tauri evidence DTO or ledger-panel changes.
Move beyond alignment/arity tuning only after a lane produces profitable exact hits or repeatable prefix>=5 movement.
Do not promote the frozen transform-validation matrix without exact selected spans after transform metadata is charged.
Stop broadening selected affine/periodic/composed prefix-4 leads unless a new lead crosses prefix>=5 or exact-hit thresholds.
Find a new lead before spending on broad depth-3 sweeps; the bounded follow-up on current promoted rows found zero selected spans.
Use the generated vocabulary-disjoint shadow corpora and binary TLV/varint controls before trusting literal-token-driven prefix wins.
Treat the corpus generalization null as a reason to implement record-aware transforms before broadening expensive corpus discovery.
Treat the BWT/MTF null as evidence that classic entropy preconditioners do not automatically create Lotus seed-span hits.
Treat the grammar/channel null as a reason to design stronger parser-backed channels before increasing seed depth.
Treat the numeric value-channel null as evidence against simple parsed-value streams before adding format support.
Use the held-out corpus expansion as a promotion gate before mutating the expensive corpus matrix or transform-validation matrix.
Treat the record/context transform null as a reason to find stronger record descriptors before spending on format support.
Treat the token/dictionary transform null as evidence against simple lexeme dictionaries without stronger grammar models.
Extend seed-depth-3 economics beyond tiny controls only after runtime and memory stay bounded.
Replace research-only GPU fallback with real OpenCL only if it beats CPU streaming under parity tests.
Push generated scale sweeps past 16 MiB after memory telemetry and chunking are acceptable.
